vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1491: feat(flywheel): metric-aware prioritization — weight substrate-bug suggestions by which v1.0 criterion they affect

plan.title: feat(flywheel): metric-aware prioritization — weight substrate-bug suggestions by which v1.0 criterion they affect
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The flywheel ranks suggestions by P-level + age alone. Substrate bugs all bucket as P1 with no signal about which one is closest to a v1.0 criterion threshold (bug discovery rate, intervention rate, flake rate, …). Operators have to mentally re-rank suggestions every tick by reading vision.mdx, which is exactly the kind of friction the flywheel is supposed to remove.
narratives.Proposal: Each substrate bug declares affected v1.0 criteria via a `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1,5` body trailer (with `affects-criterion-N` label as a fallback). The server computes a per-bug weight against the rolling 30-day metric snapshot (FlywheelStats), publishes weights through a new endpoint and CLI command, and surfaces them on FlywheelSuggestion. P0 still trumps everything; weight is a secondary sort within non-urgent priority tiers. The orchestrator (`roles/flywheel.md`) is instructed to call `pan flywheel weights --json` once per tick and merge weights into its emitted suggestions. The UI shows the weight and a one-line reason so operators see WHY the suggestion is ranked where it is.

autoDecisions.summary: Use a body trailer (`Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1,5`) as the primary mapping mechanism, with `affects-criterion-N` labels as a fallback that union with the trailer.
autoDecisions.rationale: Trailers compose with PAN-1487's planned `Flywheel-*` provenance trailers and are machine-parseable from a single `gh issue view --json body` call. Labels are operator-friendly but lossy (no comma-list, no rationale line). Supporting both lets operators tag via the GitHub UI while keeping the canonical record in the body — matches the established pattern for other Panopticon trailers.

autoDecisions.summary: Distance weight scales by metric type: rate/duration → `max(0, (value - target)/target)`; ratio-higher-is-better (c3 passRate) → `max(0, (target - value)/target)`; absolute count (c2 p0Bugs) → `value` directly.
autoDecisions.rationale: Avoids division-by-zero when targets are very small (rate criteria can reach 0). Keeps weight bounded near 0 for criteria already meeting target. Absolute counts (open P0s) cannot be normalized against a typically-zero target, so we use the raw count — one open P0 is intrinsically weightier than a 0.1% rate drift.

autoDecisions.summary: Status multiplier: red=3, yellow=2, green=1, insufficient_data=0.
autoDecisions.rationale: Multiplier on top of distance lets a yellow criterion that drifts further still outrank a red criterion drifting less. `insufficient_data=0` means we never let noise drive prioritization.

autoDecisions.summary: P0 (`urgent`) suggestions ignore weight entirely; weight breaks ties within `high`, `medium`, `low`. Operator-injected suggestions (no server-computed weight) preserve their explicit order.
autoDecisions.rationale: Preserves the existing escape hatch (PRD line: 'operators can still inject any issue manually'). Avoids the failure mode where a high-weight bug at P2 outranks a P1 incident.

autoDecisions.summary: Extend `FlywheelSuggestion` with optional `weight: number` and `weightReason: string` fields; do not add a new top-level array.
autoDecisions.rationale: Existing pipeline already consumes `FlywheelSuggestion[]` end-to-end (server → contract → frontend → orchestrator emit). Adding optional fields is a backward-compatible contract bump that any consumer can ignore.

autoDecisions.summary: Expose weights through `GET /api/flywheel/substrate-bug-weights?window=30d` and a thin `pan flywheel weights` CLI wrapper.
autoDecisions.rationale: Mirrors the existing flywheel stats endpoint + `pan flywheel status` shape so the orchestrator can call it the same way it already calls other flywheel endpoints. CLI is the orchestrator-facing surface; the route is for the dashboard.

autoDecisions.summary: Ship independently of PAN-1487. Parser tolerates absent trailers/labels and returns zero weight in that case.
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1487 is an explicit dependency on the issue but blocking on it would idle a ready-to-build feature. Zero-weight default keeps behavior identical to today for un-tagged bugs while the new sort key activates the moment a bug is tagged.

items.title: Contract: extend FlywheelSuggestion with optional weight + weightReason

narrative.Action: Add optional `weight: number` and `weightReason: string` fields to both the `FlywheelSuggestion` TypeScript interface and the Effect Schema struct in `packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts`. Rebuild the contracts package. Bump the contract change comment if one exists; do not bump the package version (workspace dependency).

subItems.title: `FlywheelSuggestion` interface in `packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts` declares `weight?: number` and `weightReason?: string`
The `FlywheelSuggestion` Effect Schema struct declares `weight: Schema.optional(Schema.Number)` and `weightReason: Schema.optional(Schema.String)` and round-trips both through `encode`/`decode`
`cd packages/contracts && npm run build` completes cleanly and `npm run typecheck` from the workspace root passes

Existing FlywheelSuggestion tests in `packages/contracts/src/flywheel.test.ts` still pass; one new test asserts a decoded `{ action, rationale, priority, weight, weightReason }` payload preserves both new fields

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/flywheel-affected-criteria.ts` exporting `parseAffectedCriteria(body: string, labels: readonly string[]): number[]`. Parser recognises a `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1,5,7` trailer (case-insensitive key, comma- or space-separated list of digits 1–7) anywhere in the body, plus any GitHub label matching `/^affects-criterion-([1-7])$/`. Result is the deduped sorted union, clamped to the 1–7 range. Empty input → `[]`. Co-locate unit tests in `src/lib/flywheel-affected-criteria.test.ts`.

subItems.title: `parseAffectedCriteria('Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1,5', [])` → `[1, 5]`
`parseAffectedCriteria('', ['affects-criterion-3', 'affects-criterion-5'])` → `[3, 5]`
Trailer + label union: body `'Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1'` with labels `['affects-criterion-5']` → `[1, 5]` (sorted, deduped)
Out-of-range or non-numeric ids are dropped; e.g., trailer `'1,8,foo'` → `[1]`
Empty body + empty labels → `[]`; never throws

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/flywheel-bug-weight.ts` exporting `computeSubstrateBugWeight(criteriaIds: readonly number[], stats: FlywheelStats): { weight: number; reason: string }`. For each criterion id 1..7 look up the corresponding `FlywheelStatsCriterion` (c1_bugRate, …, c7_flake). Apply: distance per criterion shape (rate/duration → `(value - target)/target`, ratio-higher-is-better → `(target - value)/target`, absolute count → `value`), clamped at zero. Status multiplier: red=3, yellow=2, green=1, insufficient_data=0. Resolve the per-criterion shape from the criterion's `value`/`target` JSON schema (rate/duration vs ratio vs count). Sum the contributions to produce `weight` (rounded to 2 decimals). Reason is a short sentence naming the top-contributing criterion, its current value, target, and status. Empty `criteriaIds` → `{ weight: 0, reason: 'no affected criteria declared' }`. Co-locate unit tests.

subItems.title: Bug affecting only c1 with c1.status=red, value=0.032, target=0.02 → weight ≈ (0.032-0.02)/0.02 × 3 = 1.80
Bug affecting c3 (passRate) with status=yellow, value=0.985, target=0.995 → weight ≈ (0.995-0.985)/0.995 × 2 ≈ 0.02
Bug affecting c2 (open P0s) with status=red, value=3 → weight ≈ 3 × 3 = 9 (absolute-count path)
insufficient_data criterion contributes zero even if value/target imply distance
Empty criteriaIds → `{ weight: 0, reason: 'no affected criteria declared' }` (never throws on missing telemetry)
`reason` names the top contributor by absolute contribution, formatted like `'criterion 1 (bug rate) at 3.2% vs target <2% — red'`

narrative.Action: Add a service `src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-bug-weights.ts` that joins open substrate bugs (from `flywheel-substrate-bugs-db.ts`) with their issue bodies+labels (via the existing GitHub issue cache or a `gh issue view --json body,labels` call), runs `parseAffectedCriteria` + `computeSubstrateBugWeight` against the latest `FlywheelStats` for the requested window, and returns `{ window, generatedAt, weights: [{ issueId, criteria, weight, reason }] }`. Wire `GET /api/flywheel/substrate-bug-weights?window=30d` in `src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts`. Reuse the existing stats caching layer; do not add a new cache.

subItems.title: `GET /api/flywheel/substrate-bug-weights` (no params) returns 200 with shape `{ window, generatedAt, weights: [...] }` and `window` defaulting to `'30d'`
Each entry contains `issueId: string`, `criteria: number[]`, `weight: number`, `reason: string`
Bugs whose bodies/labels declare no criteria appear in the result with `criteria: []`, `weight: 0`, `reason: 'no affected criteria declared'` — they are NOT filtered out

Route is covered by an Effect route test that mocks the substrate-bugs table and stats service and asserts ordering by weight descending within the response (frontend re-sorts but server provides the authoritative ranking)
No `execSync`/`readFileSync`/`writeFileSync` in the new service or route — uses `execAsync` and `fs/promises` per CLAUDE.md

narrative.Action: Add `pan flywheel weights [--issue <id>] [--window 30d] [--json]` to the existing `pan flywheel` subcommand group. Thin client over `GET /api/flywheel/substrate-bug-weights`. Default output is a fixed-width table (issueId, criteria, weight, reason). `--issue` filters to one bug; `--json` emits the raw payload. Update the `/pan-flywheel` skill if needed so the wrapper documents the new subcommand.

subItems.title: `pan flywheel weights --help` documents `--issue`, `--window`, `--json`
`pan flywheel weights --json` emits the unmodified server payload to stdout (parseable as JSON)
`pan flywheel weights --issue PAN-1234` returns a single-row table (or empty if not a known open substrate bug)
Skill lint (`scripts/lint-skills.sh` via `npm run lint`) passes — every flag the skill mentions matches `pan flywheel weights --help`

items.title: Prompt: update roles/flywheel.md + docs/flywheel-brief.md ranking guidance

narrative.Action: Update `roles/flywheel.md` (Inventory + Emit-suggestions steps) and `docs/flywheel-brief.md` (Scope section) so the orchestrator: (1) calls `pan flywheel weights --json` once per tick after inventory, (2) merges `weight` + `weightReason` onto matching substrate-bug suggestions, (3) sorts within each non-urgent priority tier by weight descending. Include a worked example (criterion 1 at 3.2% / target <2% / red → weight ≈ 1.8 → outranks an older P1 bug tagged criterion 3 at 99.5% / target ≥99.5% / green → weight 0). Preserve every existing constraint (eltmon author/assignee gate, `needs-design`/`needs-discussion` exclusion, operator-injected escape hatch).

subItems.title: `roles/flywheel.md` Inventory step instructs calling `pan flywheel weights --json` and explains the weight/reason fields
Emit-suggestions step documents the secondary sort by weight (descending) within `high`/`medium`/`low`, and notes that `urgent` is unaffected
`docs/flywheel-brief.md` Scope section reflects the new ranking with the same worked example
Operator-injected suggestions (no server-set weight) explicitly preserve their order — called out in both files

items.title: UI: surface weight badge + reason in FlywheelStatusDetails

narrative.Action: Update `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx` so each suggestion row optionally renders a small numeric weight badge (1-decimal) and the `weightReason` string under the rationale. Add a secondary sort within priority groups by weight descending (server provides the source-of-truth ordering, but the page re-sorts after WS event merges).

subItems.title: Suggestions with `weight` undefined render exactly as today (no badge, no reason line)
Suggestions with `weight` defined render a badge showing weight to 1 decimal place and the `weightReason` string
Within each priority tier, weighted suggestions sort by weight descending; un-weighted suggestions keep their server order
Existing FlywheelStatusDetails snapshot/unit tests still pass; one new test exercises a mixed (weighted + un-weighted) suggestion list

items.title: Hook: emit Flywheel-Affects-Criterion when filing substrate bugs

narrative.Action: When a substrate bug is filed by the flywheel (via `gh issue create` per `roles/flywheel.md`) and attribution data identifies one or more affected v1.0 criteria, append a `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: <ids>` trailer to the issue body before submission. If the affected criterion cannot be inferred, do nothing (no trailer is better than a wrong trailer). Coordinate with PAN-1487's trailer convention if it has merged.

subItems.title: `roles/flywheel.md` substrate-bug filing instructions explicitly tell the orchestrator to append `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion:` when known, with examples mapped from common bug categories (review flake → 5,7; merge regression → 4,5; planning misfire → 5; agent crash recovery → 5)

If a `gh-issue-trailer-hook` script exists, it is extended to recognise `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion:` and pass it through unmodified; if no hook exists, this acceptance criterion is satisfied by the prompt update alone (no new infra)
If attribution cannot infer a criterion, no trailer is emitted (parser tolerates absence per b2-parser.ac5)

items.title: Docs: update FLYWHEEL.md + vision links for metric-aware ranking

narrative.Action: Update `docs/FLYWHEEL.md` 'Reading the stats panel' section to document how `weight` and `weightReason` map to v1.0 criteria. Cross-link from the FLYWHEEL-VISION criteria section to this explainer. Do not duplicate content — link, don't copy.

subItems.title: `docs/FLYWHEEL.md` documents the `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion:` trailer, the `affects-criterion-N` label fallback, and how to read the weight/reason on a suggestion
`docs/FLYWHEEL-VISION.md` (or its equivalent live doc) links to the FLYWHEEL.md weight explainer from the v1.0 criteria section
No other docs duplicate the weight formula — single source of truth lives in `docs/FLYWHEEL.md`
